relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Trusting Jesus as Messiah marks birth from Divine and love for the born ones.
Love for Divine and children is known through command-keeping.
The commands of love are not heavy.
Trust overcomes the world-system.
The one trusting the Son overcomes.
Jesus Messiah comes through water and blood, with Spirit testifying truth.
Three bear witness.
Spirit, water, and blood agree as one witness.
Divine witness is greater than human witness.
The witness lives within the one trusting the Son.
The witness is age-long life in the Son.
Having the Son is having the life.
These words give knowing of age-long life.
Boldness asks according to Divine will.
Being heard becomes confidence.
Prayer seeks life for the sibling in misalignment.
All injustice is misalignment, yet not all is unto death.
The born one is kept from the harmful touch.
The community belongs to Divine even while the world-system lies in harm.
The Son gives understanding to know the True One.
Little children, guard yourselves from idols.

contemplative_note: 1 John 5 closes with witness and life: Spirit, water, and blood testify; love’s commands are not heavy; age-long life is in the Son.
